prompt:
It is important for every student to pass exams but sometimes schools pay to much attention to them. Now I am going to prove my point.
First example is a fact that in high school students are mostly studying topics which are useful for graduating exams. This means that other topics are not so widely studied. And it is really sad because different students would need different material in future and may not even need exam topics.

Another example is the fact, that lots of schools pay too much attention to solving tasks from exams. This means that student have to do similar tasks again and again instead of doing different ones and learning something new. And what is even more insane schools usually give for this process whole 11th grade and sometimes even 10th. And it is not all: in 9th grade there are also exams, which means that nearly 3 last grades are given for exams.

That why I think that schools pay too much attention to preparing students for exams.

TASK: In your Telegram group students are discussing this question:  How to study well at MIPT? Write your answer to this question. Give 3-4  recommendations.
STUDENT_TEXT: I think that for study well at MIPT is important to study a lot. You must offen lerning fisics and match. I recomend for everybody practis sport and read books. I know that the study at MIPT is very hard, but is important to finish this university. I can say, that for good studing you can ask help to his friends. For conclusion i want to recomend sleep and eat good.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hi, i had a simular problem. Here is the tips that helped me.
1) Manage your time! Use special apps and calendars. You can find this apps at Play Market.

2) Don't spend a lot of time to TV and social media. You should leave from useless groups at VK, don't watch YouTube a lot!

3) Sleep more than 8 hours and eat good food. It will bring you enought energy.

I hope, my tips will help you. For more information, you can write me at personal messages.

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hi, I know some people who have the simular problem. So, let me try to give you few tips about solving it.

Firstly, the most important thing is time managment. You need to understand when you sleep, eat, go for a walk, etc.

Then, I think it's very nice to solve problems when they was given for you. Don't put them closely to deadlines. If you don't do it, you will have to make many tasks in one-two days. It's surely not good.

My final tip is about eating healthy food. Yes, this tip is really obvios, but without it you probably will have a lot of health difficulties.

So, I hope my tips will help you to get more free time! And it's interesting for me to know, what do you think about it?

TASK: You read this post in the Telegram chat of first year students: It is so difficult to study in university! I don't have any free time at all! Write an answer to this post (10 sentences) and give some tips on how to organise your days to have more free time.
STUDENT_TEXT: Hello, friend!

You don't need to worry about studying at university so bad. You can easily become better at organizing your time. Let me give you a few tips how to do that right.

1. You should think about making day schedules. It may be very useful to think about organizing your next day routine every evening. You'll feel more in control of your actions and choices. 

2. Don't forget to move all the time. You can do some special sports or just basic physical excersices. It is very important for your health and well-being. It'll reduce your stress level as well, so you'll feel more confident at making decisions.

3. Sleep well. It's neccessary to have a good rest after a day of hard work. Try to sleep at least 8 hours each day. You'll be able to do your tasks more productively this way, so you'll have more free time as well.

As you see, these tips aren't complicated at all. I hope they'll help you to become better at your studying routine. Good luck with that!

TASK: Some people think that parents should teach their children how to be good members of society. Others, however, believe that school is the best place to learn this. Discuss both views and give your own opinion.
STUDENT_TEXT: Some individuals believe that the right place to teach children how to become good citizens is the school, while others argue that parents should be the ones responsible for that. Although parents might influence their children more than anyone else, I believe that educational institutions are more trained and equipped to teach children how to become successful members of the community. Parents influence their children more than anyone else. This is due to the fact that mothers and fathers are the ones who raise and spend most of the time with their children which dramatically influences the way children act and think. If parents act in a good manner, their children will indirectly imitate them. This fortifies the fact that no one might exert such a strong influence on their children. For example, a study in Britain showed that children are two times more influenced by their parents than their teachers. However, I believe that this is not enough and that school should be the place teaching children to become good people in society.Schools are trained to build good citizens. Teachers spent their undergraduate years studying how to deal with children and train them to become better individuals in their communities. For this reason, educational institutions should be the place where children can safely acquire the needed behaviors to become better individuals in the future. For example, a recent study in the USA showed that 90% of schools train teachers how to help students to become better citizens. For this reason, I believe that the best place to do this is the school.In conclusion, although parents have a strong influence on their children, I believe that the best place to create better citizens is the school because tutors are trained to do that.

TASK: There is an opinion that too many young people now want to get a university degree. It would be better for society if most of them start working after finishing secondary school. How much do you agree with this point of view? State your opinion and give at least two arguments to support it.
STUDENT_TEXT: The contrast between1899 and 1999 is unfathomable. The 20th century is perhaps the century of trial and error, as the humankind went crazy with change; change came in many forms, some of them brought blessing, others brought fire, but many brought both. One of the particular reasons for this was the increasing literacy and education among the population. Common folk, not just the town dandies, suddenly recognized that they have huge potential and rights, which are not to be infringed. This dash for knowledge motivated an immense interest in formal education - primary, secondary and higher, which is still ongoing today. Although primary and secondary education are widely considered compulsory (even legislatively, in most countries), the higher education is still under question. Does everyone need the degree? Personally, I do not believe so. 

Firstly, manual labor is still very important. This trivial thought often misses the brightest of minds. The World doesn't run on sole theory. Moreover, not every person can be happy at a desk with a logarithmic ruler in hand. They might be much happier with manual labor in open air in their workshop. We should incentivize these people to follow their dream and benefit the society the way they deem optimal.

Secondly, the overflow of students who get a degree just for the sake of it does not benefit the academic world. I believe that the academic world should be shaped by people who want to shape it, not forced into it by their surrounding. It takes very specific character traits to be an eager scholar. People who lack those traits, but forced into being a scholar, are unhappy themselves and are hurtful to the Academia. 

All in all, we shall preserve the diversity of professions and encourage people to follow their dreams and talents, not an abstract notion of a "degree". All professions are needed and valued, as a Soviet poem goes.
